candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A summary row above operating profit, but the selected COGS reference bucket is 'Operating expenses', which appears to be a parent total that already includes this line.
Component of the D&A summary row and therefore already covered by the parent 'Operating expenses' total; also subordinate to the summary D&A row.
Component of the D&A summary row and therefore already covered by the parent 'Operating expenses' total; also subordinate to the summary D&A row.
Looks like a component within total depreciation/D&A, not a separate add-on outside the selected parent operating-expense bucket.

variables.notes: Conservative no-add. Because the selected COGS bucket is the parent row 'Operating expenses', separate D&A rows shown beneath it appear embedded within that selected total. Adding any D&A row separately would likely double count.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A summary row at the same general operating-section level as other expense lines, but it is a company-wide operating D&A total and not clearly identifiable as SG&A-only versus production/COGS-related.
Component of the total D&A row; even less suitable than the summary row because it would risk partial and misclassified capture.
Component of the total D&A row; not clearly SG&A-specific.
Likely a component of depreciation/D&A, but not clearly tied specifically to SG&A rather than broader operating functions.

variables.notes: No separate SG&A-specific D&A row is visible. The only explicit D&A rows are broad operating totals/components and cannot be cleanly attributed to the selected XSGA bucket(s) without risking misallocation or double counting. Manual review is appropriate if a fuller statement or note disclosure can split D&A between production and SG&A.
